evidence_quotes.quote: Second area of interest is preservation of video games as digital goods.

paragraphs.text: I consider that there are 2 additional areas of interest that could be covered by this initiative: Payment processor overreach and Preservation of video games First area of interest is the currently active issue of payment processors overreaching their influence, and by doing so restricting the sale of "risky" materials on the internet. This is primarily focused on adult content that is available on the internet and includes the sale of various adult media and video games. Considering that primary way to buy things over the internet is by using one the 2 dominant payment processors, they effectively have a duopoly over shopping on the internet. As such, they indirectly have the power to censor various areas of interest on the internet. Certain areas are indeed very problematic, but by using some of the methods they are currently using, they end up hurting the consumer market and cause loss for people creating in the affected field. Second area of interest is preservation of video games as digital goods. Current state of this market is such that the publishers of video game keep pushing the boundaries of what it means to "sell a video game". They failed to, or rather it seems that they ignored, the fact that they have crossed this boundary. Game publishers are becoming more predatory and anti-consumer as time passes. More detailed explanation of this problem can be found at "European Citizens' Initiative" under the initiative name "Stop Destroying Videogames"
